user_query:
I’m setting up a small, reproducible local pipeline to collect a few official policy or guidance pages about the use of digital technology in U.S. legal proceedings for a methods appendix in my research. Please build an environment and runnable script that:

- Reads search queries, domain constraints, and keywords from input/config.yaml.
- Performs live web search engine queries (do not hardcode any target URLs) to find policy/guidance pages on official judiciary domains that discuss electronic filing, remote or virtual proceedings, or related technology.
- Selects at least two distinct pages meeting both categories below, with a maximum of four pages in total:
  1) at least one page from the Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts domain (uscourts.gov), and
  2) at least one page from a U.S. state judiciary website on a .gov domain whose hostname contains "court" or "courts" (e.g., matches the regex provided in the config).
- Downloads each selected HTML page and saves the raw content under outputs/raw/ using a safe filename derived from the URL (e.g., outputs/raw/<slug>.html).
- Extracts webpage content (title and visible text), computes total word_count of the visible text, counts occurrences of the configured keywords, detects whether the page contains links to PDFs, and records the source_url and domain.
- Produces two structured artifacts:
  1) outputs/structured/policies.jsonl — one JSON object per line with the fields: source_url (string), domain (string), category ("federal"|"state"), page_title (string or null), word_count (integer), keyword_hits (object mapping keyword->integer), has_pdf_links (boolean), local_html_path (string), fetched_at (ISO8601 UTC string).
  2) outputs/structured/manifest.json — a JSON array where each item records: source_url, local_html_path, category, fetched_at, and sha256 (hex digest) of the saved raw HTML file, to allow cross-checking.

Environment and run expectations:
- Provide requirements.txt and a single runner script scripts/run.sh (POSIX shell) that creates a local virtual environment (e.g., .venv), installs dependencies from requirements.txt, and executes the fetch/extract script end-to-end to populate outputs/.
- Implement scripts/fetch_policies.py (or similar) that performs the live web searches using the queries from input/config.yaml, filters by the specified domain constraints, fetches the pages, extracts content, and writes the outputs exactly to the paths specified above.
- The script must not hardcode any specific target URLs; it should derive candidate pages from live search results and then apply the filters from the config file. Skip duplicates and non-HTML content. If you find more than the configured maximum total pages, keep the first distinct matches per category.

Deliverables in this workspace:
- requirements.txt
- scripts/run.sh
- scripts/fetch_policies.py
- outputs/raw/… (downloaded HTML files)
- outputs/structured/policies.jsonl
- outputs/structured/manifest.json

Success criteria:
- Running scripts/run.sh on a fresh machine with Internet access creates the virtual environment, installs dependencies, performs live searches, downloads at least two pages meeting the category/domain constraints, and writes the raw HTML and both structured files as specified. policies.jsonl must contain at least one record from uscourts.gov and at least one record from a state judiciary .gov host containing "court" or "courts" in the hostname. manifest.json sha256 values must match the corresponding files in outputs/raw/.

Please avoid including direct URLs in the code or config; rely on the search queries and domain filters provided in input/config.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
